title: ee.repair_action_graph.v1
description: Structured graph of repair actions emitted by both `ee mesh status` (SRR6.46.4 / bd-36bbk.1.4) and `ee doctor` (SRR6.46.16 / bd-36bbk.1.16). Downstream consumers (agent harnesses, human operators, doctor renderers) parse this single shape to topologically execute remediation. The schema lives in `src/mesh/repair_action_graph.rs` and is shared by both surfaces by construction so the two consumer paths cannot drift apart.

actions.description: All repair actions in the graph. Sorted lexicographically by id for deterministic output. Each id MUST be unique within the graph; cross-references in `prerequisites` and downstream doctor `fixActionId` fields resolve against this list.

topologicallyOrderedExecution.description: Deterministic Kahn topological order: each action id is preceded by all of its prerequisites. Ties between independent actions break by `(priority, id)` so the output is byte-reproducible across runs and machines.

parallelizableGroups.description: Layered Kahn output. Layer N contains actions whose prerequisites are entirely satisfied by layers 0..N-1; within a layer all actions can run concurrently. Empty when `actions` is empty.

estimatedTotalDurationSeconds.description: Sum of `estimatedDurationSeconds` across all actions. Coarse upper bound on serial-execution wall-clock time; under parallelization the actual wall-clock will be smaller.

actionKind.description: What kind of action this is. `shell_command` runs in a shell, `ee_subcommand` invokes an `ee` subcommand the agent harness can drive directly, `external_tool` is a vendor tool the harness cannot drive (browser/GUI/portal), `manual_step` is operator-only (the `command` field is the human description; the renderer must not suggest auto-execution).

priority.description: Operator-facing priority. Drives the order of human-readable rendering and biases the topological tiebreaker: independent actions sort with `critical` first.

executionContext.description: Where the action executes. The agent harness reads this to route to the right executor; the human renderer uses it to phrase the instruction ("run in your shell" vs "run in ee"). Semantically distinct from `kind`: kind describes WHAT the action is, executionContext describes WHERE it runs.

resolvesChecks.description: Doctor check names (matching `name` in the doctor check output) that this action is expected to flip from `fail`/`warning` to `ok` on success.

preconditionsForNextActions.description: Action ids that become unblocked once this action completes. Redundant with the inverse of `prerequisites[]` but emitted explicitly because downstream renderers find the forward adjacency easier to display. The builder auto-populates this from the reverse adjacency map when the caller leaves it empty; caller-supplied values are preserved verbatim.

id.description: Unique action id within this graph. Lowercase snake_case so cross-references in `prerequisites` and doctor `fixActionId` are stable across renderers.

command.description: Command string to execute. For `kind=manual_step`, the human-facing instruction text rather than an executable.

humanReadable.description: One-line operator-facing description; what the renderer prints next to the command.

prerequisites.description: Action ids (from this same graph) that must complete first. The builder rejects unknown prerequisites with `UnknownPrerequisite { from, missing }` and rejects cyclic dependencies with `DependencyCycle`.

estimatedDurationSeconds.description: Wall-clock estimate for the action in seconds. Used to compute the envelope-level `estimatedTotalDurationSeconds` and to size a watchdog when the agent harness auto-executes.

reversible.description: Whether running this action is reversible by a deterministic reverse command. When true, `reversalCommand` SHOULD be present.

reversalCommand.description: Deterministic reversal command, present when `reversible == true` and a reversal exists. Null for irreversible actions (e.g. `tailscale logout` that requires re-authentication).

requiresUserConfirmation.description: Whether the renderer must surface an interactive confirmation prompt before running. Always true for destructive actions and for any action whose `reversible == false`.
